title: Two Sum
statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
constraints: Exactly one solution exists. Do not use the same element twice.
editorial: Track seen values in a hash map keyed by number so each complement lookup is O(1).
reference_solution: Use a HashMap from value to index while scanning once.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
statement: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day, maximize profit from one buy and one sell.
constraints: You must buy before you sell.
editorial: Keep the minimum price seen so far and update the best profit at each step.
reference_solution: Single pass with rolling minimum and max profit.

title: Valid Palindrome
statement: Determine if a string is a palindrome after converting to lowercase and removing non-alphanumeric characters.
constraints: Only alphanumeric characters count toward the palindrome check.
editorial: Use two pointers from both ends and skip characters that do not matter.
reference_solution: Two pointers with character filtering.

title: Valid Parentheses
statement: Given a string containing just the characters ()[]{} determine if the input string is valid.
constraints: Every closing bracket must match the most recent unmatched opening bracket.
editorial: A stack models the nesting order directly.
reference_solution: Push opening brackets and validate closers against the top.

title: Contains Duplicate
statement: Return true if any value appears at least twice in the array.
constraints: Array length may be large enough that quadratic scans are too slow.
editorial: A set detects repeats in linear time.
reference_solution: Compare set size with array size or early-return on repeated insert.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
statement: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: Need the best window length over the whole string.
editorial: Maintain a sliding window with last seen positions to shrink past duplicates.
reference_solution: Sliding window using a map of character to latest index.

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
statement: Return the length of the longest substring that can be made of one repeating character after at most k replacements.
constraints: The window is valid when window length minus the count of the most frequent character is at most k.
editorial: Track character counts and the max frequency within a sliding window.
reference_solution: Sliding window with frequency array and stale max frequency optimization.

title: Permutation in String
statement: Return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1.
constraints: Window length must match s1 length exactly.
editorial: Compare frequency counts across a fixed-size sliding window.
reference_solution: Track counts for both strings and slide one character at a time.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
statement: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array in O(log n) time.
constraints: The array was originally sorted in ascending order and contains distinct values.
editorial: At least one half is still sorted, which lets binary search discard half each step.
reference_solution: Binary search that inspects which half is sorted before choosing the next bounds.

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
statement: Find the minimum element in a rotated sorted array of unique elements.
constraints: Need O(log n) time.
editorial: Compare mid against the right edge to tell where the unsorted pivot side lives.
reference_solution: Binary search on the pivot side until left meets right.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
statement: Determine if a binary tree is a valid binary search tree.
constraints: Every node must respect all ancestor bounds, not just its direct parent.
editorial: Carry valid lower and upper bounds through DFS recursion.
reference_solution: Recursive DFS with min and max bounds.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
statement: Return the level order traversal of a binary tree's node values.
constraints: Nodes should be grouped by depth from top to bottom.
editorial: Breadth-first search naturally processes nodes one level at a time.
reference_solution: Use a queue and process each level by current queue size.

title: Number of Islands
statement: Count the number of islands in a 2D grid of water and land cells.
constraints: Adjacent land is connected horizontally or vertically.
editorial: Each DFS or BFS marks one whole connected component.
reference_solution: Scan the grid and flood-fill each new land cell.

title: Clone Graph
statement: Return a deep copy of a connected undirected graph.
constraints: Every original node should map to exactly one cloned node.
editorial: A hash map prevents cloning the same node twice while traversing neighbors.
reference_solution: DFS or BFS with original-node to clone-node map.

title: Course Schedule
statement: Determine if you can finish all courses given prerequisite pairs.
constraints: A cycle makes completion impossible.
editorial: Detect a cycle in the prerequisite graph with DFS states or topological ordering.
reference_solution: Use Kahn's algorithm or DFS visitation states to detect cycles.

title: Maximum Subarray
statement: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum.
constraints: At least one number exists.
editorial: At each index decide whether extending the current run beats restarting here.
reference_solution: Kadane's algorithm with rolling best ending here.

title: Climbing Stairs
statement: How many distinct ways can you climb to the top if you can take 1 or 2 steps?
constraints: Recurrence depends only on the previous two states.
editorial: This is Fibonacci in disguise: ways[n] = ways[n-1] + ways[n-2].
reference_solution: Iterative DP with two rolling variables.

title: Coin Change
statement: Return the fewest number of coins needed to make up a given amount.
constraints: If the amount cannot be formed, return -1.
editorial: Build the best answer bottom-up for every sub-amount up to the target.
reference_solution: Bottom-up DP over amounts with coin transitions.

title: Container With Most Water
statement: Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that holds the most water.
constraints: Area is limited by the shorter line times width.
editorial: Move the shorter side inward because moving the taller side cannot improve the bottleneck height.
reference_solution: Two pointers shrinking inward from both ends.

title: 3Sum
statement: Return all unique triplets in the array which give the sum of zero.
constraints: Triplets must be unique, and the naive cubic scan is too slow.
editorial: Sort once, fix one value, then use a two-pointer sweep for the remaining pair.
reference_solution: Sort and run two pointers for each anchor while skipping duplicates.
